behavior_in_task_description.reason: The instruction specifies all file paths (header.css, footer.css, main.css, css.html, js.html), required content (border-bottom, margin-bottom, border-top, margin-top, @import statements), exact option patterns like `(cond hugo.IsDevelopment false true)` and `(cond hugo.IsDevelopment "linked" "none")`, and constraints like no targetPath or "external" in js.html. All test assertions map to explicitly stated requirements.

no_solution_leakage.reason: The instruction specifies the exact solution: exact file contents to create, exact template option values like `(cond hugo.IsDevelopment false true)`, exact @import syntax, and exact constraints. It tells the agent precisely WHAT to write rather than describing broken behavior to diagnose. For example, 'Configure the minify option with the value (cond hugo.IsDevelopment false true)' is literally the code to write.

solution_uniqueness_guard.reason: test_js_html_uses_cond_dict_pattern asserts exact strings like `(cond hugo.IsDevelopment false true)` and `(cond hugo.IsDevelopment "linked" "none")`. An alternative correct implementation using e.g. `(not hugo.IsDevelopment)` for minify or `(if hugo.IsDevelopment "linked" "none")` would fail these tests despite being functionally equivalent.

tests_verify_behavior_not_text.reason: While several tests check file content via string matching, there are also substantial behavioral tests: test_repo_go_build compiles Hugo, test_repo_go_vet runs go vet, test_repo_gofmt checks formatting, test_repo_css_build_tests and test_repo_create_package_tests run unit test suites, and test_skeleton_templates_valid builds a site with the skeleton theme in both development and production modes. Not every test is pure text matching.

test_not_tautological.reason: The file-content tests require specific files to exist with specific content (e.g., header.css must contain border-bottom). A stub/empty implementation would fail because the files don't exist by default. The build tests (go build, go test) would also fail if the skeleton is malformed. These cannot be satisfied by default/empty implementations.

behavior_in_tests.reason: All behavior specified in instruction.md is tested: component CSS files existence and content, main.css imports and absence of inline styles, css.html and js.html template options, environment-aware configuration values, and successful Hugo builds.

informative_test_structure.reason: Tests are well-named (test_header_css_exists, test_main_css_uses_imports, test_css_html_uses_css_build, etc.), have docstrings explaining purpose, and are logically grouped from file existence checks to content validation to build verification.

structured_data_schema.reason: This task produces CSS files, HTML templates, and build artifacts — not structured data like JSON/CSV/API/DB.

no_typos.reason: All file paths, commands, variable names, and option names in the instruction are consistent with the test expectations and solution. No typos detected.

instruction_no_hint_leakage.reason: The instruction specifies exact option values like `(cond hugo.IsDevelopment false true)` and `(cond hugo.IsDevelopment "linked" "none")`, exact @import syntax, and exact constraints on what must/must not be in each file. This goes beyond describing what's broken and tells the agent exactly what code to write.

config_edit_scope_bounded.reason: This is not an agentmd-edits task; it involves creating and modifying CSS, HTML template, and configuration files.
